Let's dig right into it right now here. I'm going to show you some very, very powerful tonalities on film here and you'll see that when I say something one way it has one impact and when I say it another way it has quite a different impact, in fact quite more profound impact. Now in fact there are eight crucial tonal patterns in all these eight tones that you must learn to muster at any given moment while you're influencing, while you're persuading. And we're going to learn each one of those ad noisiness and make sure that you master them at the highest level so they actually become part of you. And as I said earlier this is not as difficult as it sounds to you because you've already used them. You see at one point in your life you used each of these tonalities in normal course of conversation. The difference is that in the past you weren't consciously aware of precisely what they could do and precisely when to use them. Now with the straight line we're going to actually make them conscious, we're going to actually dissect them out, make them ingrained into your DNA and then with the tactical part of the straight line we'll show you precisely when to use them and how to get the greatest impact from them. Now there's one more point here I want to get into. It's very important here before I move on to the exact tonal patterns and we start to really send them home. And that point is this issue of logic versus emotion when people are making decisions. There's an old adage people say well you know people make their decisions logically or people buy and make decisions emotionally. The truth is both of them are crucial. You see ultimately people buy on emotion but they justify their decisions with logic. If either one of them is missing it's very, very tough to close at the highest level. So what we learn to do with the straight line is move people both logically and emotionally sometimes at the same time, sometimes at separate times but ultimately be wrapped up in such a way that as we get to that point of absolute certainty bam it's both on a logical and an emotional level. Now here's the key. Words typically the words that we say all what move people logically. They're facts, they're figures, they're things that we can say hmm okay I heard what he said and yes it does add up or no it does not add up. So the words that we say is typically what moves people logically while we're selling. But what moves them emotionally is our tonality. It's how we say it.
